name: Route of the Embalse del Molino de Chincha (PR-CU 92)
name_original: Ruta del Embalse del Molino de Chincha (PR-CU 92)
region_intro: Hiking around Cañizares offers access to the mountainous terrain of the Cuenca Mountains, characterized by spectacular rock formations, ravines, and deep river gorges. The region features lush pine forests and a verdant environment sustained by rivers like the Escabas and Júcar. Hikers can explore unique karst landscapes and distinctive limestone formations. This area provides diverse natural features for outdoor activities. Best hiking trails around Cañizares The most popular hiking route is El Cucurucho and La Hocecilla Trail, a 7.6 miles (12.3 km) trail that takes 3 hours 51 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers views of the Cuenca mountain range. Another top favourite among local hikers is Route of the Embalse del Molino de Chincha (PR-CU 92), a moderate 6.2 miles (10.0 km) path. This trail winds around the Embalse del Molino de Chincha reservoir. Local hikers also love the Cascada Campana del Tío Mil Hombres – circular from Fuertescusa via Cañizar trail (SL-CU 13), a 4.1 miles (6.6 km) trail leading through varied forest terrain to a waterfall, often completed in about 1 hour 53 minutes. Hiking around Cañizares is defined by mountainous terrain, river gorges, and lush pine forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy paths to more challenging ascents. The routes in Cañizares are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 70 reviews. More than 600 hikers have used komoot to explore Cañizares's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Cañizares?
A: Cañizares offers a wide variety of hiking opportunities, with over 50 trails recorded on komoot. These routes cater to different skill levels, including 19 easy, 31 moderate, and 3 difficult options, ensuring there's a suitable path for every hiker.

Q: Are there any easy, family-friendly hikes in Cañizares?
A: Yes, Cañizares has several easy trails perfect for families. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013693821/natural-pool-of-canamares-circular-by-the-escaba-river-and-la-dehesa-camping" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural pool of Cañamares - circular by the Escaba river and La Dehesa camping&lt;/a&gt;. This easy 3.4 km route takes about an hour to complete and explores the natural pool and the Escaba river, offering a pleasant experience for all ages.

Q: What kind of terrain and landscapes can I expect on hikes in Cañizares?
A: Hiking in Cañizares means exploring the mountainous terrain of the Cuenca Mountains. You'll encounter spectacular rock formations, deep river gorges like the Escabas, and lush pine forests. The region is also known for its unique karst landscapes and distinctive limestone formations, providing diverse and breathtaking scenery.

Q: Are there any trails that feature waterfalls or significant water features?
A: Absolutely. The region is rich in water features. You can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013697635/cascada-campana-del-tio-mil-hombres-circular-from-fuertescusa-via-canizar-trail-sl-cu-13" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascada Campana del Tío Mil Hombres – circular from Fuertescusa via Cañizar trail (SL-CU 13)&lt;/a&gt;, which leads through varied forest terrain to a beautiful waterfall. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4740317" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Cuervo River&lt;/a&gt; is a captivating spectacle with waterfalls and crystal-clear pools.

Q: Where can I find panoramic views or viewpoints during my hike?
A: For stunning panoramic views, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1010282474/hoz-de-tragavivos-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hoz de Tragavivos Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which leads to the spectacular Tragavivos Viewpoint. Other notable spots include the Alto de Peñajosa (Peña el Reloj viewpoint) for vistas of the Cuenca mountain range and the Escabas river gorge, and the Ventano del Diablo (Devil's Window) overlooking the Júcar river canyon.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the area?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013697635/cascada-campana-del-tio-mil-hombres-circular-from-fuertescusa-via-canizar-trail-sl-cu-13" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascada Campana del Tío Mil Hombres – circular from Fuertescusa via Cañizar trail (SL-CU 13)&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 6.6 km loop. Another easy circular path is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013693821/natural-pool-of-canamares-circular-by-the-escaba-river-and-la-dehesa-camping" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural pool of Cañamares - circular by the Escaba river and La Dehesa camping&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Cañizares?
A: The routes in Cañizares are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 70 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse terrain, from river gorges to pine forests, and the well-maintained paths that offer options for various ability levels.

Q: Are there any unique geological formations or landmarks to explore?
A: Cañizares is renowned for its fascinating karst landscapes. A prime example is the Ciudad Encantada ("Enchanted City"), a geological park with limestone formations resembling a mystical city. You can also explore the Cueva del Hierro (Iron Cave) for impressive stalactites and stalagmites.

Q: Can I find trails that pass by lakes or reservoirs?
A: Certainly. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013360295/route-of-the-embalse-del-molino-de-chincha-pr-cu-92" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Embalse del Molino de Chincha (PR-CU 92)&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate trail that winds around the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5400732" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Molino de Chincha Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. You can also visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5070576" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna Grande de El Tobar&lt;/a&gt;, a beautiful lake in the vicinity.

Q: What wildlife might I encounter while hiking in Cañizares?
A: The diverse environment of Cañizares supports a variety of wildlife. The lush woods are home to deer, roe deer, and wild boars. You might also spot various birds, including vultures and other raptors, especially in the more remote mountainous areas.

Q: Is there a good time of year to visit Cañizares for hiking?
A: The region's climate makes it suitable for hiking during much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for exploring the trails and enjoying the vibrant flora. Summer can be warm, but the abundance of water features and shaded forests can provide relief. Winter hikes are also possible, especially on lower elevation trails.

Q: Are there any trails that offer a mix of nature and art?
A: Yes, the Ruta de las Caras (Faces of Buendía) is a unique route that combines nature with art. It features giant sculptures carved into rock faces, offering an artistic contrast to the natural surroundings and a memorable hiking experience.
tips: Upon arrival at the Strait of Guijarro, the Guadiela River was channeled forming the Molino de Chincha reservoir, a paradise with turquoise waters that can be traveled along its eastern bank, an itinerary with beautiful panoramic views in every corner.
